reason_for_decision: The article passes because it grounds RF bearing trust in site physics, calibration, multipath, and validation rather than in nominal angular accuracy alone.
strengths: The article is strong on showing why site geometry and multipath often matter more than a nominal brochure angle number.
It explains calibration and array integrity in a way that remains readable while still feeling technically credible.
The acceptance-testing section gives clear buyer and commissioning guidance instead of stopping at theory.
summary_assessment: This is a strong applied knowledge-base article. It reads like practical engineering guidance and gives the reader a credible framework for judging RF bearing quality in deployment.
